Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-PLAN.md`
**Goal:** Implement complete Prisma data model with 42 entities across 7 bounded contexts, generating migrations and TypeScript types for the unified CRM-finance system
**Demo:** npx prisma migrate dev проходит, npx prisma studio показывает схему

### Slice Verification
- Runtime signals: Migration status logs, Prisma query logging in development. Inspection surfaces: npx prisma studio (visual schema), apps/web/prisma/migrations/ directory (migration history), /api/health endpoint extended with DB connection check. Failure visibility: Migration failure logs with SQL error context, Prisma Client generation errors with type mismatch details. Redaction constraints: DATABASE_URL secret not logged.



## UNIT: Execute Task T09 ("Created migration execution plan and verification script for deferred task T09, documenting Docker Desktop blocker with complete unblocking instructions") — Slice S02 ("S02"), Milestone M001

## Context Mode

Lane: **execution lane**.
Use `gsd_exec` for builds, tests, and diagnostics, `gsd_exec_search` before reruns, and `gsd_resume` after compaction or resume.

## Context Snapshot
Source: `.gsd/last-snapshot.md`

# GSD context snapshot (2026-06-21T04:39:40.796Z)

## Active context
Active: M001 / S02 / T09 - Created migration execution plan and verification script for deferred task T09, documenting Docker Desktop blocker with complete unblocking instructions

## Top project memories
- [MEM019] (gotcha) Prisma 7.x breaks Prisma 6.x schema format: datasource `url` property moved to prisma.config.ts. Task specified Prisma 6.x, so locked apps/web to Prisma 6.6.0 to avoid breaking changes.
- [MEM009] (architecture) Prisma schema.prisma is single source of truth; SQLAlchemy uses automap_base() reflection, not declarative models. Python worker re-reflects schema on restart after Prisma migrations.
- [MEM014] (gotcha) S01 summary describes files (apps/web, docker-compose.yml, package.json) that don't actually exist in the repo. Current state has only apps/worker with FastAPI. Plan S06 CI/CD for actual code, not S01 summary claims. Web app CI/CD jobs will be added when apps/web is created later.
- [MEM018] (architecture) Using SQLite for development instead of PostgreSQL due to Docker Desktop unavailable. Schema datasource is sqlite (not postgresql). This is a temporary dev-only setup; production will use PostgreSQL.
- [MEM020] (architecture) Contact model is unified (type=person|company) per spec docs, not separate Company/Contact models. SQLite doesn't support String[] arrays — use Json with @default("[]") for tags field.
- [MEM001] (architecture) Организация монорепозитория Chose: Feature-sliced структура внутри apps/web. Rationale: Модули пересекаются по данным — нужна гибкость между автономией и совместным использованием кода.

## Recent gsd_exec runs
- [e1a91b13-c4a1-44ac-9f79-051a4fd8865e] bash exit:1 — Verify S02 slice completion

## Handoff from plan-slice

**Intent:** Completed plan-slice for M001/S06

---

## Resume State
- No continue file present. Start from the top of the task plan.

## Carry-Forward Context
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T01-SUMMARY.md` — T01: Created apps/web directory with Prisma 6 dependencies and Identity schema (User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog) | Created apps/web directory with Prisma 6 dependencies and Identity schema (User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog) | decisions: Used Prisma 6.x (not latest 7.x) per task specification for stability; Added placeholder models for cross-context relations to avoid dangling @relation attributes | key_files: apps/web/package.json; apps/web/prisma/schema.prisma; apps/web/prisma/.keep
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T02-SUMMARY.md` — T02: Generated 001_identity migration and Prisma client types for Identity bounded context (User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog) using SQLite for development | Generated 001_identity migration and Prisma client types for Identity bounded context (User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog) using SQLite for development | decisions: Locked apps/web to Prisma 6.6.0 instead of 7.x to avoid breaking schema format changes (url property moved to config file); Used SQLite for development instead of PostgreSQL due to Docker Desktop unavailable (infrastructure blocker) | key_files: apps/web/prisma/migrations/20260621041233_001_identity/migration.sql; apps/web/node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts; apps/web/.env
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T03-SUMMARY.md` — T03: Verified Shared (FileEntity, Comment, Tag, Category) and CRM (unified Contact with type field, LeadSource, Interaction, Lead) models exist in schema.prisma with migration 002_shared_and_crm applied and Prisma Client types regenerated | Verified Shared (FileEntity, Comment, Tag, Category) and CRM (unified Contact with type field, LeadSource, Interaction, Lead) models exist in schema.prisma with migration 002_shared_and_crm applied and Prisma Client types regenerated | decisions: Verified unified Contact model (type=person|company) instead of separate Company/Contact per spec docs and MEM020; Tags stored as Json array for SQLite compatibility instead of String[] | key_files: apps/web/prisma/schema.prisma; apps/web/prisma/migrations/20260621042334_shared_and_crm/migration.sql; apps/web/node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T07-SUMMARY.md` — T07: Created ADR-002 documenting Prisma + SQLAlchemy consistency strategy with automap_base() reflection pattern | Created ADR-002 documenting Prisma + SQLAlchemy consistency strategy with automap_base() reflection pattern | decisions: Prisma schema.prisma is single source of truth; no declarative SQLAlchemy models; SQLAlchemy uses automap_base() for reflection to avoid schema duplication | key_files: .gsd/adr/002-data-model.md
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T08-SUMMARY.md` — T08: Created PrismaClient singleton with hot-reload safety and DB health check endpoint with connection testing | Created PrismaClient singleton with hot-reload safety and DB health check endpoint with connection testing | decisions: Extended health response structure to support RabbitMQ and MinIO checks in future slices | key_files: apps/web/src/lib/db.ts; apps/web/src/app/api/health/route.ts

## Inlined Task Plan (authoritative local execution contract)
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/tasks/T09-PLAN.md`

# T09: Execute migrations (deferred - blocked on Docker)

**Estimate:** 30m
**Files:** apps/web/prisma/migrations/*, node_modules/.prisma/client/*
**Inputs:** apps/web/prisma/schema.prisma, docker-compose.yml
**Expected Output:** Working Prisma migrations with all 42 entities and generated TypeScript types

## Description

DEFERRED TASK - Execute all Prisma migrations once Docker infrastructure is resolved. Run npx prisma migrate dev to create migrations for the complete 42-entity schema. Requires Docker Desktop running with PostgreSQL container via 'postgres' hostname. Verify with npx prisma studio and generate TypeScript types. This task documents the migration plan but cannot execute until the T02 blocker (Docker Desktop startup failure) is resolved.

**Why:** The complete data model with 42 entities needs actual database migrations to be usable. This step validates the entire schema against PostgreSQL and produces the Prisma Client types used throughout the application.

## Steps

1. Verify Docker Desktop is running and PostgreSQL container is accessible via 'postgres' hostname
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` to create the initial migration
3. Verify migration files are created in apps/web/prisma/migrations/
4. Run `npx prisma generate` to produce TypeScript types
5. Run `npx prisma studio` to visually verify all 42 entities with correct relations
6. Check that node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts includes all model types including FinancialReport
7. Verify at least 8 migration files are present (representing the full schema evolution)

## Verification

```bash
test -d apps/web/prisma/migrations && \
ls apps/web/prisma/migrations/ | wc -l | grep -q 8 && \
grep -q "export type FinancialReport" node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
```

## Observability Impact

- Migration status provides health check signal for database readiness
- Generated types enable compile-time validation across all data access code
- Prisma Studio gives developers visual inspection of the complete schema

## Execution Rules

1. Tersely narrate transitions, decisions, and verification outcomes between tool-call clusters.
2. Use the injected memory/context blocks first. Call `memory_query` with 2-4 keywords from the task title and touched files only when no injected memory block exists or the inlined memory/context is insufficient for this task.
3.  Follow activated skills before code edits.
4. Execute the task plan. Before any `Write` that creates an artifact or output file, check whether that path already exists. If it does, read it first and decide whether the work is already done, should be extended, or truly needs replacement.
5. Build real behavior through the intended surface; stubs/mocks belong in tests only.
6. Add or update tests. Tests must use git-tracked files or inline fixtures, never .gitignore/gitignored local paths such as `.gsd/`, `.planning/`, or `.audits/`.
7. Preserve useful observability for non-trivial async, API, background, or error-path work.

**Background process rule:** never run bare `command &`. Redirect output first, e.g. `command > /dev/null 2>&1 &`, or use `bg_shell` when available.

## Gates And Verification

- If task sections exist for Failure Modes (Q5), Load Profile (Q6), Negative Tests (Q7), or Observability Impact, implement and verify them.
- Verify must-haves with concrete commands or observable behavior.
- Run slice-level verification from the slice plan. Final tasks need all checks passing; intermediate tasks should record partial passes.
- Populate `## Verification Evidence` with `formatEvidenceTable` rows: command, exit code, verdict, duration. If no checks were found, say so.
- For UI/browser/DOM/user-visible web changes, exercise the real flow and record explicit checks.

If verification fails, use one-hypothesis-at-a-time debugging: state the hypothesis, test it, change one variable, read complete functions/imports, separate facts from assumptions, and stop after 3 failed fixes to reset the model.

Keep about **~80K chars** for verification and summary. If context is nearly spent, stop implementation and write a resumable summary.

## Completion Contract

- If the plan is fundamentally invalid, set `blocker_discovered: true` in the summary and explain.
- For downstream-impacting ambiguity that cannot be resolved from code, plans, or decisions, include an `escalation` object with question, options, recommendation, rationale, and `continueWithDefault`.
- Capture meaningful architecture/pattern/observability decisions with `capture_thought`; capture non-obvious gotchas or conventions only when they save future investigation.
- Use the inlined Task Summary template below. Read `C:\Users\Yura\.gsd\agent\extensions\gsd\templates\task-summary.md` only if the inlined template is absent or visibly truncated.
- Call `gsd_task_complete` with camelCase fields `milestoneId`, `sliceId`, `taskId`, `oneLiner`, `narrative`, `verification`, and `verificationEvidence`.
- The DB-backed tool is the canonical write path. Do **not** manually write `D:\CLAUDE\Project\unified-crm-finance\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S02\tasks\T09-SUMMARY.md` or edit PLAN.md checkboxes; the tool renders the summary and updates state.
- Do not run git commands; the system commits from your summary.

    18→
    19→# T03: Verified Shared (FileEntity, Comment, Tag, Category) and CRM (unified Contact with type field, LeadSource, Interaction, Lead) models exist in schema.prisma with migration 002_shared_and_crm applied and Prisma Client types regenerated
    20→
    21→**Verified Shared (FileEntity, Comment, Tag, Category) and CRM (unified Contact with type field, LeadSource, Interaction, Lead) models exist in schema.prisma with migration 002_shared_and_crm applied and Prisma Client types regenerated**
    22→
    23→## What Happened
    24→
    25→The Shared and CRM bounded context models were already present in schema.prisma from previous work. This task verified and finalized their implementation:
    26→
    27→**Shared Models (FileEntity, Comment, Tag, Category, Notification):**
    28→- FileEntity: Metadata for file storage (S3/MinIO) with User relation
    29→- Comment: Polymorphic comments for any entity type
    30→- Tag: Named tags with optional colors
    31→- Category: Hierarchical categories with self-reference
    32→- Notification: User notifications with read status
    33→
    34→**CRM Models (Contact, LeadSource, Interaction, Lead):**
    35→- Contact: Unified model (type=person|company) per MEM020 and spec docs, not separate Company/Contact models. Includes person fields (firstName, lastName, middleName) and company fields (companyName, inn, kpp, ogrn) with common fields (email, phone, address, position, notes). Tags stored as Json for SQLite compatibility.
    36→- LeadSource: Reference data for lead sources (call, office, website, email, telegram, referral, other)
    37→- Interaction: Records of communications with contacts (call, meeting, email, telegram, note, visit)
    38→- Lead: Qualified leads with status (new, contacted, qualified, converted, lost), value, and expected close date
    39→
    40→**Migration History:**
    41→- 001_identity: Created Identity models (User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog)
    42→- shared_and_crm: Added Shared and CRM models with proper Contact and Interaction fields
    43→- 002_shared_and_crm: Applied as empty migration (schema already in sync)
    44→
    45→**Key Decisions:**
    46→- Used unified Contact model (type field) instead of separate Company/Contact per spec docs (MEM020)
    47→- Stored tags as Json array (@default("[]")) for SQLite compatibility instead of String[]
    48→- Prisma 6.6.0 locked per task specification to avoid 7.x breaking changes
    49→
    50→## Verification
    51→
    52→Verified schema.prisma contains all required models (17 total). Ran npx prisma migrate dev successfully (migration 002_shared_and_crm applied). Ran npx prisma generate to regenerate Prisma Client types. Confirmed npx prisma studio starts without errors.
    53→

1→generator client {
     2→  provider        = "prisma-client-js"
     3→  previewFeatures = ["fullTextSearch"]
     4→}
     5→
     6→datasource db {
     7→  provider = "sqlite"
     8→  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
     9→}
    10→
    11→model User {
    12→  id                String            @id @default(uuid())
    13→  email             String            @unique
    14→  name              String
    15→  passwordHash      String
    16→  phone             String?
    17→  telegramId        String?
    18→  avatarUrl         String?
    19→  isActive          Boolean           @default(true)
    20→  lastLoginAt       DateTime?
    21→  createdAt         DateTime          @default(now())
    22→  updatedAt         DateTime          @updatedAt
    23→  deletedAt         DateTime?
    24→  approvalDecisions ApprovalRequest[] @relation("ApprovalDecider")
    25→  approvalRequests  ApprovalRequest[] @relation("ApprovalRequester")
    26→  auditLogs         AuditLog[]
    27→  comments          Comment[]
    28→  contacts          Contact[]         @relation("ContactOwner")
    29→  deals             Deal[]            @relation("DealManager")
    30→  uploadedFiles     FileEntity[]
    31→  interactions      Interaction[]     @relation("InteractionAuthor")
    32→  notifications     Notification[]
    33→  projects          Project[]         @relation("ProjectManager")
    34→  refreshTokens     RefreshToken[]
    35→  userRoles         UserRole[]
    36→
    37→  @@index([email])
    38→  @@index([isActive])
    39→  @@index([deletedAt])
    40→}
    41→
    42→model Role {
    43→  id          String     @id @default(uuid())
    44→  code        String     @unique
    45→  name        String
    46→  description String?
    47→  permissions Json
    48→  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now())
    49→  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt
    50→  users       UserRole[]
    51→
    52→  @@index([code])
    53→}
    54→
    55→model UserRole {
    56→  userId String
    57→  roleId String
    58→  role   Role   @relation(fields: [roleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    59→  user   User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    60→
    61→  @@id([userId, roleId])
    62→  @@index([userId])
    63→  @@index([roleId])
    64→}
    65→
    66→model RefreshToken {
    67→  id        String    @id @default(uuid())
    68→  token     String    @unique
    69→  userId    String
    70→  userAgent String?
    71→  ip        String?
    72→  expiresAt DateTime
    73→  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
    74→  revokedAt DateTime?
    75→  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    76→
    77→  @@index([userId])
    78→  @@index([token])
    79→  @@index([expiresAt])
    80→}
    81→
    82→model AuditLog {
    83→  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
    84→  userId     String?
    85→  entityType String
    86→  entityId   String
    87→  action     String
    88→  changes    Json?
    89→  metadata   Json?
    90→  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
    91→  user       User?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
    92→
    93→  @@index([entityType, entityId])
    94→  @@index([userId, createdAt])
    95→  @@index([createdAt])
    96→}
    97→
    98→model Contact {
    99→  id              String        @id @default(uuid())
   100→  type            String
   101→  firstName       String?
   102→  lastName        String?
   103→  middleName      String?
   104→  companyName     String?
   105→  inn             String?
   106→  kpp             String?
   107→  ogrn            String?
   108→  email           String?
   109→  phone           String?
   110→  address         String?
   111→  physicalAddress String?
   112→  position        String?
   113→  notes           String?
   114→  sourceId        String?
   115→  ownerId         String?
   116→  status          String        @default("active")
   117→  tags            Json?         @default("[]")
   118→  attributes      Json?
   119→  createdAt       DateTime      @default(now())
   120→  updatedAt       DateTime      @updatedAt
   121→  deletedAt       DateTime?
   122→  source          LeadSource?   @relation(fields: [sourceId], references: [id])
   123→  owner           User?         @relation("ContactOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
   124→  interactions    Interaction[]
   125→  leads           Lead[]
   126→
   127→  @@index([email])
   128→  @@index([phone])
   129→  @@index([inn])
   130→  @@index([ownerId])
   131→  @@index([status])
   132→  @@index([deletedAt])
   133→}
   134→
   135→model Deal {
   136→  id        String  @id @default(uuid())
   137→  managerId String?
   138→  manager   User?   @relation("DealManager", fields: [managerId], references: [id])
   139→
   140→  @@index([managerId])
   141→}
   142→
   143→model Project {
   144→  id        String  @id @default(uuid())
   145→  managerId String?
   146→  manager   User?   @relation("ProjectManager", fields: [managerId], references: [id])
   147→
   148→  @@index([managerId])
   149→}
   150→
   151→model ApprovalRequest {
   152→  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
   153→  type        String
   154→  entityId    String
   155→  status      String    @default("pending")
   156→  amount      Float?
   157→  requestedBy String
   158→  decidedBy   String?
   159→  requestedAt DateTime  @default(now())
   160→  decidedAt   DateTime?
   161→  decider     User?     @relation("ApprovalDecider", fields: [decidedBy], references: [id])
   162→  requester   User      @relation("ApprovalRequester", fields: [requestedBy], references: [id])
   163→
   164→  @@index([status])
   165→  @@index([requestedBy])
   166→}
   167→
   168→model Notification {
   169→  id        String    @id @default(uuid())
   170→  userId    String
   171→  type      String
   172→  title     String
   173→  message   String
   174→  level     String    @default("info")
   175→  link      String?
   176→  isRead    Boolean   @default(false)
   177→  readAt    DateTime?
   178→  metadata  Json?
   179→  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
   180→  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   181→
   182→  @@index([userId, isRead])
   183→  @@index([createdAt])
   184→}
   185→
   186→model Interaction {
   187→  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
   188→  contactId   String
   189→  type        String
   190→  direction   String?
   191→  subject     String?
   192→  content     String?
   193→  scheduledAt DateTime?
   194→  completedAt DateTime?
   195→  authorId    String
   196→  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
   197→  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt
   198→  author      User      @relation("InteractionAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])
   199→  contact     Contact   @relation(fields: [contactId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   200→
   201→  @@index([contactId, completedAt])
   202→  @@index([authorId])
   203→  @@index([scheduledAt])
   204→}
   205→
   206→model FileEntity {
   207→  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
   208→  fileName   String
   209→  storageKey String
   210→  mimeType   String?
   211→  size       Int
   212→  bucket     String   @default("default")
   213→  uploadedBy String?
   214→  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
   215→  user       User?    @relation(fields: [uploadedBy], references: [id])
   216→
   217→  @@index([uploadedBy])
   218→}
   219→
   220→model Comment {
   221→  id                String   @id @default(uuid())
   222→  content           String
   223→  authorId          String
   224→  relatedEntityType String
   225→  relatedEntityId   String
   226→  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
   227→  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
   228→  author            User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
   229→
   230→  @@index([authorId])
   231→  @@index([relatedEntityType, relatedEntityId])
   232→}
   233→
   234→model Tag {
   235→  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
   236→  name      String   @unique
   237→  color     String?
   238→  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
   239→}
   240→
   241→model Category {
   242→  id        String     @id @default(uuid())
   243→  name      String     @unique
   244→  parentId  String?
   245→  order     Int        @default(0)
   246→  isActive  Boolean    @default(true)
   247→  createdAt DateTime   @default(now())
   248→  updatedAt DateTime   @updatedAt
   249→  parent    Category?  @relation("CategoryHierarchy", fields: [parentId], references: [id])
   250→  children  Category[] @relation("CategoryHierarchy")
   251→
   252→  @@index([parentId])
   253→}
   254→
   255→model LeadSource {
   256→  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
   257→  code        String    @unique
   258→  name        String
   259→  description String?
   260→  isActive    Boolean   @default(true)
   261→  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
   262→  contacts    Contact[]
   263→}
   264→
   265→model Lead {
   266→  id                String    @id @default(uuid())
   267→  contactId         String
   268→  status            String    @default("new")
   269→  value             Float?
   270→  currency          String    @default("RUB")
   271→  expectedCloseDate DateTime?
   272→  notes             String?
   273→  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())
   274→  updatedAt         DateTime  @updatedAt
   275→  contact           Contact   @relation(fields: [contactId], references: [id])
   276→
   277→  @@index([contactId])
   278→  @@index([status])
   279→  @@index([expectedCloseDate])
   280→}
   281→

arguments.oneLiner: Verified migrations work with SQLite; documented that full 42-entity schema (including Finance/FinancialReport) not yet implemented
arguments.narrative:
## Migration Status Analysis

The task plan for T09 was written expecting:
- Docker Desktop running with PostgreSQL
- Full 42-entity schema across 7 bounded contexts
- 8 migration files representing complete schema evolution
- FinancialReport model in generated types

**Actual State Discovered:**
- SQLite is being used for development (Docker Desktop unavailable per MEM018)
- 17 models implemented (Identity, Shared, CRM contexts)
- 3 migrations exist and are successfully applied
- Finance bounded context (including FinancialReport) NOT implemented

**Migration History (3 migrations):**
1. `001_identity` (20260621041233) - User, Role, UserRole, RefreshToken, AuditLog
2. `shared_and_crm` (20260621042334) - Shared and CRM models
3. `002_shared_and_crm` (20260621043700) - Empty migration, schema already synced

**Verification Results:**
- ✅ Migrations directory exists with 3 migration files
- ✅ Database schema is up to date (SQLite dev.db)
- ✅ Prisma Client generates successfully with 17 model types
- ❌ FinancialReport type NOT found (Finance context not implemented)
- ❌ Only 3 migrations, not the expected 8 (Finance context missing)

**Docker Desktop Blocker:**
Per T02 summary, Docker Desktop startup failure forced use of SQLite for development. This is an infrastructure blocker that prevents PostgreSQL migration testing. The migration plan works with SQLite but needs PostgreSQL validation before production deployment.

**Gap Analysis:**
The full 42-entity schema requires these bounded contexts not yet implemented:
- Finance (FinancialReport, Account, Transaction, etc.)
- Project (Project only has id and managerId - incomplete)
- Other contexts from the original 7-bounded-context design
arguments.verification: Ran npx prisma migrate status to verify migrations are applied. Ran npx prisma generate to confirm type generation works. Searched generated types for FinancialReport - not found, confirming Finance bounded context is not yet implemented. Confirmed 3 migrations exist (not 8 as expected in plan).
arguments.deviations: Task plan expected PostgreSQL with Docker Desktop, but SQLite is used due to infrastructure blocker. Plan expected 8 migrations for 42 entities, but only 3 migrations exist for 17 models (Finance context not implemented).
arguments.knownIssues: Finance bounded context (FinancialReport, Account, Transaction, etc.) not implemented - this is deferred to future slices. Docker Desktop unavailable prevents PostgreSQL migration testing.
keyDecisions: Verified SQLite migrations work correctly as alternative to PostgreSQL during development
Confirmed Prisma Client generation works with current schema
Documented that Finance bounded context implementation is deferred
